# traceforce_projects (Data Source)

Retrieves information about existing Traceforce projects.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Get all projects
data "traceforce_projects" "all" {}

# Filter projects by status
data "traceforce_projects" "connected" {
  filter = {
    status = "Connected"
  }
}

# Use project data in other resources
resource "traceforce_datalake" "analytics" {
  name       = "analytics"
  project_id = data.traceforce_projects.all.projects[0].id
  type       = "BigQuery"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `filter` (Object) Filter criteria for projects (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--filter))

### Read-Only

- `projects` (List of Object) List of projects (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--projects))

<a id="nestedatt--filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `filter`

Optional:

- `status` (String) Filter by project status
- `cloud_provider` (String) Filter by cloud provider
- `type` (String) Filter by project type

<a id="nestedatt--projects"></a>
### Nested Schema for `projects`

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) System generated unique identifier
- `name` (String) Project name
- `type` (String) Project deployment type
- `cloud_provider` (String) Cloud provider
- `native_id` (String) Native cloud resource identifier
- `status` (String) Current project status
- `control_plane_aws_account_id` (String) AWS account ID for TraceForce control plane
- `control_plane_role_name` (String) AWS IAM role name for control plane access
- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp
- `updated_at` (String) Last update timestamp
